steps.text: Take the positive square root (all variables are positive): $x = \frac{\sqrt{7fg}}{10}$. Now evaluate each choice.

choices.explanation: • $x = \frac{\sqrt{7fg}}{10}$ matches $x = \frac{\sqrt{7fg}}{10}$ exactly. That matches choice A — A is correct.
• $x = 10\sqrt{7fg}$ would give $x^{2} = 700fg$, but we need $x^{2} = \frac{7fg}{100}$. The 10 belongs in the denominator, not multiplied on top. Not correct.
• $x = \sqrt{\frac{7fg}{10}}$ would give $x^{2} = \frac{7fg}{10}$, but the denominator should be 100 (since $(10x)^{2}$ contributes a factor of $10^{2}$). Not correct.
• $x = \sqrt{7fg} - 10$ squaring gives $7fg - 20\sqrt{7fg} + 100$, which cannot equal $\frac{7fg}{100}$ in general. Not correct.
